title: parasenecio dissectus
intro: Parasenecio dissectus, also known as dissected fernleaf ragwort, is a perennial herb in the sunflower family native to the mountainous regions of Mexico and Central America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds that may help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The plant's health benefits include its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Parasenecio dissectus often involve infusions and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
